name: GetResourcesJson — public translation bootstrap
pseudocode: // GetAll: checks cache 'sms.lsr.all.{languageId}'; if miss, SELECT * FROM LocaleStringResources WHERE LanguageId = @id
IF resource.ResourceName NOT IN result THEN
// Duplicate guard is defensive; DB unique index prevents duplicates in practice
notes: Key upper-casing matches BiTranslateService which also upper-cases all lookup keys.
No HTTP-level caching headers — browser will cache based on server defaults.
Called once per language per Angular app bootstrap (page load / language switch).

name: Resources — paginated admin search
pseudocode: FUNCTION Resources(languageId: int, query: LanguageResourcesQuery) → DataTableResult<LanguageResourceModel>
// GetIncomplete: CTE finds ResourceNames with any 'Missing Translation' version, returns all rows for matching names
data = filtered.Paginate(query)  // applies .Skip(first).Take(rows), optional sort by sortField/sortOrder
RETURN 200 Ok({ Total: total, Data: data })
notes: Filtering is in-memory (post-DB load) — full dataset is loaded then filtered. Acceptable for the expected dataset size (translations rarely exceed 10k rows).
ResourceValue in model has double-backslash replaced with single-backslash before returning.

name: InsertLocalizedResource — create new translation key
pseudocode: FUNCTION InsertLocalizedResource(resourceName: string, resourceValue: string, languageId: int = 1)
IF resourceName empty OR resourceValue empty THEN return 400
IF existingValue NOT empty AND existingValue != resourceName THEN return 400 'Resource already exists'
// Note: GetLocalizedResource returns resourceName itself when not found (fail-open), so existingValue == resourceName means not-found
CREATE locale = new LocaleStringResource { LanguageId, ResourceName = resourceName.Trim(), ResourceValue }
FOR EACH otherLanguage IN GetAllLanguages() WHERE Id != locale.LanguageId:
IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM LocaleStringResources WHERE LanguageId = otherLanguage.Id AND ResourceName = locale.ResourceName) THEN
notes: The 'already exists' check uses fail-open behaviour of GetLocalizedResource — counter-intuitive but correct.
Auto-creates placeholder rows for ALL other languages — maintains ResourceName completeness invariant.
Frontend tap() in insertLocalizedResources correctly calls getLanguageCodeByLanguageId() to update ngx-translate cache.

name: UpdateLocalizedResource — edit existing translation
pseudocode: FUNCTION UpdateLocalizedResource(id: int, resourceName: string, resourceValue: string, languageId: int)
IF id == 0 OR resourceName empty OR resourceValue empty THEN return 400
IF locale == null THEN return 400 'Resource not found'
UPDATE LocaleStringResources SET ResourceName=@name, ResourceValue=@value, LanguageId=@langId WHERE Id=@id
notes: Updates all three fields — allows renaming ResourceName, changing LanguageId assignment, or editing the value.
Does NOT cascade ResourceName rename to other languages — if you rename 'foo.bar' to 'foo.baz', other language rows still have 'foo.bar'. Risk: silent orphan creation.
GAP_005: frontend cache update is buggy for non-DK/SE languages.

name: BulkMerge import (batch CLI)
pseudocode: // NOTE: no cacheManager.RemoveByPattern() called here — GAP_002
notes: GAP_002: cache not invalidated after import. Imported translations invisible until cache expires or app restarts.
BulkMerge = UPSERT: existing rows updated, new rows inserted. No deletes.
